_meta.source: Per-county / per-city assessor publications. Rates reflect the EFFECTIVE NEW-BUYER rate at the representative metro: base ad-valorem + voter-approved bonds + school district levies + typical special assessments. Deliberately NOT the Tax Foundation state-average rate, which is tenure-weighted and understates new-buyer bills in jurisdictions with assessment caps (CA Prop 13, FL SOH, TX 10% caps).
_meta.schema: rate is effective decimal, city is the metro the rate represents, notes describes what's included, source is the canonical assessor URL

AL.notes: Jefferson County millage at 15% assessment ratio for residential.

AK.notes: Anchorage areawide mill rate ~17.5 mills; assessed at 100% of market value.

AZ.notes: Maricopa County primary + secondary rate at 10% assessment ratio for residential, including school district overrides.

AR.notes: Pulaski County millage at 20% assessment ratio.

CA.notes: 1% Prop 13 base + ~0.25% LA County / LAUSD voter-approved bonds and direct assessments. Mello-Roos neighborhoods can run 1.5%+. SF is similar at ~1.18%.

CO.notes: Denver County mill levy at the 6.7% residential assessment rate (Gallagher Amendment fallout).

CT.notes: CT mill rates × 70% assessment ratio. Stamford ~0.74%, but state-typical for a new buyer in a metro is ~1.85% (West Hartford 1.85%, New Haven 2.5%, Hartford 4.6%). Stamford is the low end.

DE.notes: Wilmington city + New Castle County + school district. DE assessments are decades-old which deflates effective rate; reassessment underway.

DC.notes: DC residential Class 1 rate is $0.85/$100 of assessed value (assessed at full market). Homestead deduction available but not modeled.

FL.notes: Miami-Dade aggregate millage ~21 mills. Save Our Homes cap NOT applied (new-buyer scenario). FL counties vary 0.85%–1.20%.

GA.notes: Fulton County + Atlanta city + Atlanta Public Schools millage at 40% assessment ratio.

HI.notes: Honolulu County residential rate $2.80/$1,000 (assessed at full market). One of the lowest effective rates in the country.

ID.notes: Ada County levies at full market assessment.

IL.notes: Cook County effective rate ~2.27% for new buyers; nation-leading for major metro. Includes Chicago Public Schools, county, and city levies.

IN.notes: Marion County millage with 1% statutory cap on homestead — but new-buyer effective often hits the cap.

IA.notes: Polk County + Des Moines city + school district at 54.6% residential rollback.

KS.notes: Sedgwick County millage at 11.5% residential assessment ratio.

KY.notes: Louisville Metro + JCPS + state. Includes occupancy and library taxes.

LA.notes: Orleans Parish millage at 10% residential assessment ratio. Homestead exemption applied.

ME.notes: Portland city mill rate at full market assessment.

MD.notes: Montgomery County rate $0.989/$100 + state $0.112 + special districts.

MA.notes: Boston city is lower (0.65%) due to residential exemption; representative tech-couple metro Cambridge/Newton/Brookline ~1.05–1.10%.

MI.notes: Detroit city is 2.79% (highest big-city rate in country). Suburbs like Royal Oak / Birmingham MI ~1.85%. Tech-couple representative is suburbs.

MN.notes: Hennepin County + Minneapolis + MPS. Homestead market value exclusion applied.

MS.notes: Hinds County millage at 10% homestead assessment ratio.

MO.notes: Jackson County millage at 19% residential assessment ratio.

MT.notes: Statewide reappraisal 2023 raised effective rates significantly.

NE.notes: Douglas County + Omaha + OPS. Among highest in country.

NV.notes: Clark County millage at 35% residential assessment ratio with a 3%/yr abatement cap (not applied for new-buyer scenario).

NH.notes: Manchester city + state education property tax. NH has no income or sales tax; property carries the load.

NJ.notes: Jersey City effective ~1.50%. Newark ~2.71%, Hoboken ~1.42%. NJ statewide average is 2.21% (highest in US); JC is the tech-couple representative.

NM.notes: Bernalillo County millage at 33⅓% residential assessment ratio.

NY.notes: NYC Class 1 (1-3 family) effective ~0.88%. NYC Class 2 (condo/coop) effective higher per market value; this rate fits a new-buyer townhouse/brownstone. Westchester / Long Island suburbs run 2.0%–2.5%.

NC.notes: Mecklenburg County + Charlotte city + CMS bonds.

ND.notes: Cass County millage at full true and full value.

OH.notes: Franklin County + Columbus city + CCS bonds. Cleveland (Cuyahoga) is even higher at ~2.74%.

OK.notes: Oklahoma County millage at 11% residential assessment ratio.

OR.notes: Multnomah County + Portland city + PPS bonds. Measure 5/50 caps are mostly already binding; new-buyer effective is ~1.02%.

PA.notes: Philadelphia rate 1.3998% (city + school) of market value (10% LOOP adjustment for some). Pittsburgh ~2.16%.

RI.notes: Providence mill rate $24.56/$1,000 at full assessed value.

SC.notes: Charleston County millage at the 4% owner-occupied assessment ratio (much lower than the 6% non-owner-occupied ratio).

SD.notes: Minnehaha County + Sioux Falls + SD school formula.

TN.notes: Metro Nashville unified rate at 25% residential assessment ratio.

TX.notes: Harris County + city + HISD + emergency services. New-buyer rate before homestead cap kicks in. Austin (Travis Co) similar at 2.18%; Plano with MUD often 2.5%+.

UT.city: Salt Lake City / Salt Lake County
UT.notes: Salt Lake County millage at the 55% primary-residence reduction.

VT.notes: Chittenden County + Burlington city + state education property tax.

VA.notes: Arlington residential rate $1.013/$100 + special tax districts. Fairfax 1.115%, Alexandria 1.135%. NoVA tech-couple representative.

WA.notes: King County total levy rate at full market value, including school bonds and state property tax.

WV.notes: Kanawha County levies at 60% of market value (Class II residential).

WI.notes: Milwaukee city + MMSD + MPS. Among highest big-city rates in country.

WY.notes: Laramie County millage at 9.5% residential assessment ratio.
